gomodel:run-figure2-006/tir1/molecular_f.snippet: TIR-1 acts as a signaling adaptor in the C. elegans intestinal p38/PMK-1 immune pathway.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/nsy1/molecular_f.snippet: NSY-1 is the MAP3K (ASK1 ortholog) of the NSY-1/SEK-1/PMK-1 cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/sek1/molecular_f.snippet: SEK-1 is the MAP2K of the NSY-1/SEK-1/PMK-1 cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/pmk1/molecular_f.snippet: PMK-1 is the p38 MAP kinase of the intestinal immune cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/hsp60/molecular_.snippet: HSP-60 is the mitochondrial chaperonin; ATP-dependent protein folding chaperone activity.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/atfs1/molecular_.snippet: ATFS-1 (ATF-4/5-like bZIP) acts as the DNA-binding transcription factor of the UPRmt.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/hif1/molecular_f.snippet: HIF-1 is the hypoxia-inducible bHLH-PAS transcription factor.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/hif1/molecular_f.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-006/aak2/molecular_f.snippet: aak-2 is the catalytic subunit of C. elegans AMPK (figure node 'AMPK').

gomodel:run-figure2-006/daf16/molecular_.snippet: DAF-16/FOXO acts as a DNA-binding transcriptional activator.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/daf16/molecular_.term_label: DNA-binding transcription activator activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-006/skn1/molecular_f.snippet: SKN-1/Nrf2 is a DNA-binding transcription factor (sequence-specific DNA binding shown by IDA in PMID:28600327).

gomodel:run-figure2-006/hsf1/molecular_f.snippet: HSF-1 heat shock transcription factor; DNA-binding transcription factor activity.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/atf7/molecular_f.snippet: ATF-7 is the bZIP (ATF/CREB) transcription factor phosphorylated by PMK-1.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/zip4/molecular_f.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-006/cebp1/molecular_.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-006/zip2/molecular_f.snippet: ZIP-2 bZIP transcription factor mediating the C. elegans response to P. aeruginosa.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/zip2/molecular_f.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-006/nhr86/molecular_.snippet: NHR-86/HNF-4-like nuclear hormone receptor acting as a DNA-binding transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/fshr1/molecular_.snippet: FSHR-1 is a glycoprotein-hormone-type GPCR at the intestinal plasma membrane.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/tir1/part_of.snippet: TIR-1 is required for intestinal antibacterial defense; this BP also captures the figure's pyocyanin/Pseudomonas stimulus that activates TIR-1.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/nsy1/part_of.snippet: NSY-1 functions in the p38 (PMK-1) MAPK cascade controlling intestinal immunity.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/sek1/part_of.snippet: SEK-1 functions in the p38 (PMK-1) MAPK cascade controlling intestinal immunity.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/pmk1/part_of.snippet: PMK-1 is the terminal p38 MAP kinase of the NSY-1/SEK-1/PMK-1 cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/hsp60/part_of.snippet: HSP-60 mitochondrial chaperonin is a readout/effector of the UPRmt.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/atfs1/part_of.snippet: ATFS-1 is the master transcription factor of the UPRmt; drives nuclear stress-response gene expression.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/hif1/part_of.snippet: HIF-1 regulates RNA Pol II transcription of stress-response genes (hypoxia/iron/ROS branch in the figure).
gomodel:run-figure2-006/hif1/part_of.term_label: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:run-figure2-006/daf16/part_of.snippet: DAF-16/FOXO regulates transcription of stress-response/defence genes downstream of p38 in the figure.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/skn1/part_of.snippet: SKN-1/Nrf2 activates transcription of cellular oxidative-stress-response genes.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/skn1/part_of.term_label: positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:run-figure2-006/hsf1/part_of.snippet: HSF-1 activates transcription of stress-response (heat shock) genes.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/hsf1/part_of.term_label: positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

gomodel:run-figure2-006/atf7/part_of.snippet: PMK-1-phosphorylated ATF-7 switches to an activator driving antimicrobial defence gene transcription.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/atf7/part_of.term_label: positive regulation of innate immune response

gomodel:run-figure2-006/zip4/part_of.snippet: ZIP-4 contributes to intestinal defence against Gram-negative bacteria; regulates zip-2 output.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/cebp1/part_of.snippet: CEBP-1 contributes to intestinal defence against Gram-negative bacteria.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/zip2/part_of.snippet: ZIP-2 drives antibacterial defence/ESRE gene expression in response to P. aeruginosa.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/nhr86/part_of.snippet: NHR-86/HNF-4 drives antimicrobial defence gene transcription.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/fshr1/part_of.snippet: FSHR-1 GPCR signaling contributes to the intestinal innate immune response.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/tir1/occurs_in.snippet: TIR-1/SARM1 adaptor acts in the cytoplasm of the intestinal cell; no specific GO CC annotation retrieved.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/tir1/occurs_in.justification: TIR-1 is a cytoplasmic signaling adaptor scaffolding the NSY-1 MAP3K; cytoplasmic localization is inferred. Figure places it inside the intestinal cell (cell type could not be grounded to a CL/WBbt CURIE, so the cell-type extension is omitted).

gomodel:run-figure2-006/nsy1/occurs_in.snippet: NSY-1 MAP3K acts in the cytoplasm; no specific GO CC annotation retrieved.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/nsy1/occurs_in.justification: MAP3K of a cytoplasmic MAPK relay; cytoplasmic localization inferred. Intestinal-cell compartment from figure could not be grounded to a cell-type CURIE.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/sek1/occurs_in.snippet: SEK-1 MAP2K acts in the cytoplasm; no specific GO CC annotation retrieved.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/sek1/occurs_in.justification: MAP2K of a cytoplasmic MAPK relay; cytoplasmic localization inferred. Intestinal-cell compartment could not be grounded to a cell-type CURIE.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/pmk1/occurs_in.snippet: PMK-1 p38 MAPK acts largely in the cytoplasm (also shuttles to nucleus); no specific GO CC annotation retrieved.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/pmk1/occurs_in.justification: p38 MAPK of a cytoplasmic relay; cytoplasmic localization inferred. Intestinal-cell compartment could not be grounded to a cell-type CURIE.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/hsp60/occurs_in.snippet: HSP-60 localizes to the mitochondrion (figure: Mitochondria compartment).

gomodel:run-figure2-006/atfs1/occurs_in.snippet: ATFS-1 accumulates in the nucleus to drive UPRmt gene transcription.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/daf16/occurs_in.snippet: Active DAF-16/FOXO is nuclear; figure draws it driving nuclear stress-response genes.
gomodel:run-figure2-006/daf16/occurs_in.justification: DAF-16/FOXO acts as a nuclear transcription factor when activated; a nucleus CC annotation surely exists but was not within the retrieved annotation rows, so tagged instinct rather than citing an unseen reference.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/skn1/occurs_in.snippet: SKN-1/Nrf2 localizes to the nucleus to drive oxidative-stress-response genes.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/atf7/occurs_in.snippet: ATF-7 is a nuclear transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/zip4/occurs_in.snippet: ZIP-4 transcription factor localizes to the nucleus.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/zip2/occurs_in.snippet: ZIP-2 transcription factor localizes to the nucleus.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/nhr86/occurs_in.snippet: NHR-86 nuclear hormone receptor localizes to the nucleus.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/fshr1/occurs_in.snippet: FSHR-1 GPCR localizes to the plasma membrane (figure: transmembrane receptor at Lumen/Intestinal-cell boundary).

gomodel:run-figure2-006/tir1/causal/gomo.snippet: TIR-1 (SARM ortholog) adaptor acts upstream of the NSY-1/SEK-1/PMK-1 cascade; tir-1 product directly positively regulates NSY-1. Figure routes this via LRO stress / pH increase (TIR-1→LRO→NSY-1 collapsed to one edge).

gomodel:run-figure2-006/nsy1/causal/gomo.snippet: NSY-1 (MAP3K) phosphorylates and activates the MAP2K SEK-1; nsy-1 product directly positively regulates SEK-1.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/sek1/causal/gomo.snippet: SEK-1 (MAP2K) phosphorylates and activates the p38 MAPK PMK-1; sek-1 product directly positively regulates PMK-1.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/pmk1/causal/gomo.snippet: PMK-1 phosphorylates ATF-7, converting it from repressor to activator of innate-immune genes; pmk-1 product directly positively regulates ATF-7 (ATF-7 binds the PMK-1 MAP kinase, IPI).

Pointed arrow PMK-1 → DAF-16/FOXO: pmk-1 product is causally upstream, positive effect, of DAF-16 activity. Mechanism/directness not specified in figure; no specific regulatory PMID found, so tagged as figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/hsp60/causal/gom.snippet: Faint/uncertain pointed arrow HSP-60 → ATFS-1/ATF-4 (figure confidence ~0.35): mitochondrial-stress/chaperone state causally upstream, positive effect, of the ATFS-1 UPRmt transcription factor. Low-confidence figure read; verify direction (canonically ATFS-1 induces HSP-60).

gomodel:run-figure2-006/aak2/causal/gomo.snippet: Arrow AMPK (aak-2) → HIF-1 marked uncertain in figure (confidence ~0.35): AMPK activity causally upstream, positive effect, of HIF-1. Low-confidence figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/zip4/causal/gomo.snippet: Pointed arrow ZIP-4 → ZIP-2: ZIP-4 bZIP TF causally upstream, positive effect, of ZIP-2 activity (likely heterodimer/transcriptional co-regulation). No specific regulatory PMID confirmed; figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/cebp1/causal/gom.snippet: Pointed arrow CEBP-1/2 → ZIP-2: CEBP-1 bZIP TF causally upstream, positive effect, of ZIP-2 activity. No specific regulatory PMID confirmed; figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2-006/hif1/has_small_m.snippet: Figure: mtROS / +Fe3+ from Mitochondria → HIF-1 (uncertain routing, ~0.35). Mitochondrial reactive oxygen species act as a positive small-molecule signal increasing HIF-1 activity (canonically by stabilizing HIF-1); modeled as has small molecule activator. Figure read; verify directness.
